content.fact: At the opening of The Phantom Menace, the Trade Federation blockade has made Naboo the immediate political crisis.
content.injection: During the Naboo blockade, center political pressure on Naboo, the Trade Federation, and the Senate response rather than on open galactic war.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Naboo Crisis Begins.
Respect story-position bounds from Naboo Crisis Begins to Naboo Crisis Begins.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Jedi Act as Envoys Before the War

content.fact: Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan enter the Naboo crisis as Jedi envoys, not as commanders of a Republic army.
content.injection: Before the Clone Wars, portray Jedi intervention as diplomacy, protection, investigation, and limited combat rather than military command.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode I: The Phantom Menace.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode I: The Phantom Menace to Anakin Accepted for Jedi Training.

entries.title: Anakin Starts as an Enslaved Child on Tatooine

context.label: Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine

content.fact: When Qui-Gon meets Anakin, Anakin is an enslaved child on Tatooine with exceptional mechanical talent and unusually strong Force potential.
content.injection: In Episode I context, write Anakin as a gifted enslaved child on Tatooine, not as a trained Jedi, soldier, Sith, or adult political actor.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine.
Respect story-position bounds from Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine to Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine.

entries.title: Anakin Has Potential, Not Jedi Control

content.fact: Anakin has extraordinary Force potential in Episode I but lacks Jedi discipline and formal technique.
content.injection: Do not give Episode I Anakin reliable Jedi skills beyond instinct, reflex, empathy, and raw potential. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episode I: The Phantom Menace.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode I: The Phantom Menace to Anakin Accepted for Jedi Training.

entries.title: Qui-Gon Suspects Anakin Is the Chosen One

context.label: Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine

content.fact: Qui-Gon comes to believe Anakin may be the Chosen One, but this is contested and not institutionally accepted at first.
content.injection: Treat Qui-Gon’s belief in Anakin as the Chosen One as strong but disputed; the Jedi Council is not fully convinced by default.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine.

entries.title: Darth Maul Reveals the Sith Threat

context.label: Maul Reveals Sith Threat on Tatooine

content.fact: Darth Maul’s attack confirms that Sith activity has returned to practical relevance after long institutional disbelief.
content.injection: After Maul reveals himself, Jedi characters may treat the Sith threat as real but still poorly understood. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Naboo Victory Does Not Start Open Galactic War

content.fact: The liberation of Naboo ends the local crisis but does not yet start the Clone Wars or expose the Sith master.
content.injection: After Naboo is liberated, do not jump directly to open Republic-Separatist war unless the story advances toward Attack of the Clones or later.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Naboo Liberated.
Respect story-position bounds from Naboo Liberated to Naboo Liberated.

entries.title: Qui-Gon Dies and Obi-Wan Becomes Anakin’s Teacher

entries.title: Dooku Not Yet Public Sith After Episode I

content.injection: Do not foreground Count Dooku as the known Separatist Sith leader in immediate post-TPM scenes unless the story deliberately advances or uses private Sith knowledge.
